purpose: Implement two new flags on tool/signals_sync.hexa requested by parent cycle's tooling_carry_forward block: (a) --apply-trivial flag env-gated by NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY=1; (b) --emit-patch flag writing unified diffs per APPLY_TRIVIAL action to design/patches/signals/. Default behavior remains plan-only — no mutation without explicit env+flag combo. CROSS_PUBLISH actions deferred (require future --cross-publish flag + cross-repo sign-off; OUT OF SCOPE for THIS kick). THIS KICK BUILDS THE TOOLING; IT DOES NOT MUTATE ACTUAL n6-arch atlas.signals.n6.

tier_1_implemented.rationale: Added --apply-trivial flag with hard env-gate at main() entry: env_var('NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY') must equal exact string '1' for mutation to proceed. Any other value (unset, '0', 'yes', 'true') triggers immediate raw 66 refusal: __SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY__ FAIL applied=0 patches=0 skipped=0 reason=apply-trivial-without-env-gate followed by reason+fix trailer, exit(1). Apply path additionally re-verifies env-gate per-action defensively (mid-run flag flip yields per-id [REFUSE] + skipped++). The env-gate is genuine: there is NO backdoor, NO default-on, NO override flag. Only NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY=1 unlocks mutation.

Added --emit-patch flag generating unified-diff .patch files (one per APPLY_TRIVIAL action) via `diff -u --label <repo>:atlas.signals.n6:<sig_id>` over temp files containing the per-id entry blocks. Output directory defaults to design/patches/signals/<YYYY-MM-DD>_<sig_id>.patch with optional --patch-out <dir> override. Patch emission is DECOUPLED from apply: --emit-patch alone (no env-gate) writes patches WITHOUT mutation; --apply-trivial alone (with env-gate) applies WITHOUT writing patches; both flags together write patches AND apply. CROSS_PUBLISH ids are NOT iterated by the apply/patch pipeline (they live in only-A/only-B sets, never in common[]); explicitly tagged [SKIP action=CROSS_PUBLISH (deferred)] in pipeline log when they would appear via future surface.

falsifiers.name: apply-trivial-flag-recognized
falsifiers.criterion: --apply-trivial parses without 'unknown arg' error; combined with --emit-patch both flags coexist and trigger respective code paths
falsifiers.evidence: Smoke test: NEXUS_CORE_ROOT=/tmp/signals_apply_fake_root NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY=1 hexa run tool/signals_sync.hexa --apply-trivial --emit-patch --patch-out /tmp/signals_apply_fake_root/patches → '__SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY__ PASS applied=1 patches=1 skipped=0' on fixture. Both flags coexist.

falsifiers.name: env-gate-NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY-required-for-apply
falsifiers.criterion: Running --apply-trivial WITHOUT NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY=1 must refuse immediately with raw 66 trailer + exit(1), zero mutation
falsifiers.evidence: hexa run tool/signals_sync.hexa --apply-trivial → '__SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY__ FAIL applied=0 patches=0 skipped=0 reason=apply-trivial-without-env-gate' + 'reason=apply-trivial-without-env-gate fix=NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY=1 hexa run ...' + EXIT=1. No file touched.

falsifiers.name: emit-patch-generates-unified-diff-files
falsifiers.criterion: --emit-patch writes one valid `diff -u` formatted .patch per APPLY_TRIVIAL action, with proper --- / +++ headers and @@ hunks, to the configured output directory
falsifiers.evidence: hexa run tool/signals_sync.hexa --emit-patch --patch-out /tmp/sigs_patch_smoke (no env-gate) generated 12 .patch files matching the 12 APPLY_TRIVIAL ids identified by parent cycle's enumeration. Sample SIG-7H-001.patch shows correct unified-diff header `--- nexus:atlas.signals.n6:SIG-7H-001 / +++ n6-arch:atlas.signals.n6:SIG-7H-001` with 3-line hunk showing CROSS-tag delta and witness 4 vs 5.

falsifiers.name: default-no-mutation-when-flags-absent
falsifiers.criterion: Running with no apply/patch flags must produce ZERO mutation on either /Users/ghost/core/canon/atlas/atlas.signals.n6 or /Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.signals.n6
falsifiers.evidence: shasum -a 256 captured before+after default-mode run (no flags). n6-arch=a5f52d0c4b0f424d878a98076f31ab105ebf28b9352b13bf5bd75568cc8175db unchanged. nexus=0fcd55e0bd04406d1d4ae220c13dd52719cebf131d652fc0a446ba17961cae91 unchanged. Identical pre/post.

falsifiers.name: cross-publish-still-deferred-no-auto-apply
falsifiers.criterion: Even with NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY=1 + --apply-trivial + --emit-patch, CROSS_PUBLISH actions must NOT be applied or patched
falsifiers.evidence: Apply pipeline only iterates _intersect(A_ids, B_ids) (=common ids). CROSS_PUBLISH actions are by definition only-in-A or only-in-B and never enter the common-id loop. Fixture test with SIG-Z-001 (only-in-nexus, classified CROSS_PUBLISH) confirmed: not iterated, not applied, not patched. Only SIG-X-001 (HEADER_DRIFT → APPLY_TRIVIAL) was acted on. Pipeline header explicitly labels SKIP action=CROSS_PUBLISH if any common-id were ever (mis)classified as such.

falsifiers.name: no-regression-on-existing-signals_sync-modes
falsifiers.criterion: All pre-existing modes (--self-test, --by-id, --repos, --proposed-merge, --detail, default) continue to work identically and emit __SIGNALS_SYNC__ sentinel correctly
falsifiers.evidence: Re-ran each mode post-edit. __SIGNALS_SYNC_SELFTEST__ PASS. --by-id reports 'cross_publish=20 apply_trivial=12 propose_only=0 obstruction=0 tail_artifact=1' (matches parent cycle counts). --repos reports nexus=420 n6-arch=400 anima-n6=400. --proposed-merge prints unchanged plan block. Default summary __SIGNALS_SYNC__ PASS repos=3 drift=40 content_drift=26 unchanged.

facts: F1 PASS — --apply-trivial and --emit-patch flags are recognized; coexist correctly when combined with NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY=1.
F2 PASS — env-gate enforced: --apply-trivial without env=1 refuses with raw 66 reason=apply-trivial-without-env-gate + fix= directive; exit code 1; zero mutation.
F3 PASS — 12 unified-diff .patch files generated for the 12 APPLY_TRIVIAL ids; format verified via direct diff -u call against per-id entry blocks; --label injects repo:file:sigid identifiers.

F4 PASS — default mode (no apply/patch flags) leaves both /Users/ghost/core/canon/atlas/atlas.signals.n6 (sha=a5f52d0c4b0f424d…) and /Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.signals.n6 (sha=0fcd55e0bd04406d…) byte-identical pre/post.

F5 PASS — CROSS_PUBLISH actions never enter the apply/patch loop (lives in only-A/only-B sets, not common); fixture test with SIG-Z-001 (only-in-nexus CROSS) confirmed not-applied/not-patched even with full env+both-flags combo.
F6 PASS — all 5 pre-existing modes run unchanged. __SIGNALS_SYNC_SELFTEST__ PASS. --by-id totals match parent cycle (20/12/0/0/1). --repos / --proposed-merge / default unchanged.

Hard policy honored: real /Users/ghost/core/canon/atlas/atlas.signals.n6 NOT mutated this kick (sha verified pre+post). Real /Users/ghost/core/nexus/n6/atlas.signals.n6 NOT mutated. Apply-path was exercised ONLY against /tmp/signals_apply_fake_root fixtures via NEXUS_CORE_ROOT override.

Env-gate is genuine: no backdoor flag, no default-on path, no override mode. Source-grep confirms NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY is checked at exactly 2 sites — main() entry + per-id defensive recheck. Both require exact string '1'.

Anima copies are skipped from apply/patch pipeline (only nexus -> n6-arch direction is in scope this kick). Pipeline log emits '[apply/patch] skipping pair nexus vs anima-n6 (only nexus -> n6-arch is in scope)'.

carry_forward: Future kick: --cross-publish flag for CROSS_PUBLISH 20 actions. Requires (a) env-gate NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_PUBLISH=1, (b) per-direction explicit user authorization (nexus -> n6-arch separate from n6-arch -> nexus), (c) cross-repo sign-off mechanism (pull request or coordination atom in both repos).

Future kick: when user authorizes apply against real n6-arch, invocation is: NEXUS_SIGNALS_SYNC_APPLY=1 hexa run tool/signals_sync.hexa --apply-trivial --emit-patch (writes 12 .patch files to design/patches/signals/ AND mutates n6-arch). Patches serve as audit trail + revert basis.
Future kick: extend --apply-trivial with --dry-run sub-flag that prints 'would apply X' lines without write, even WITH env-gate. Useful for review before blessing real run.

Future kick: anima symlink propagation audit. After any real apply against n6-arch, sha-verify anima/n6/atlas.signals.n6 reflects the change (symlink should propagate transparently but worth witness-recording).
Future kick: add `--apply-trivial --batch <list-of-sigids>` to scope mutation to a user-specified subset of the 12 APPLY_TRIVIAL candidates (avoids all-or-nothing).

Future kick: per-action git commit on n6-arch side after each --apply-trivial application (one commit per @S id; commit message includes raw 66 reason=apply-trivial-authorized + ref to the .patch file).
